faq.title: What You Need to Know About Hair Transplant
faq.description: We have answered the most common questions about the hair transplant process.

items.question: What is the Sapphire method?
items.answer: The Sapphire FUE method is an advanced version of the traditional FUE technique. Channels are opened using special sapphire-tipped blades, allowing denser transplantation, reduced tissue trauma, and faster healing. It provides more natural and dense results.

items.question: What is a graft?
items.answer: A graft is a tissue unit containing hair follicles. One graft may contain between 1 and 4 hair strands. Planning is based on the number of grafts needed.

items.question: How long does a hair transplant take?
items.answer: The procedure usually takes between 6 to 8 hours depending on the number of grafts.

items.question: Is hair transplant painful?
items.answer: The procedure is performed under local anesthesia, so patients do not feel pain during the operation. Mild sensitivity may occur afterward but resolves quickly.

items.question: How is the number of grafts determined?
items.answer: The number of grafts is determined by the size of the bald area and the density of the donor region.

items.question: What is shock loss after hair transplant?
items.answer: Shock loss is the temporary shedding of transplanted hairs within 2–6 weeks. This is normal, and permanent hair growth starts around the 3rd month.

items.question: When are results visible?
items.answer: Initial growth starts around the 3rd month. Noticeable density appears by the 6th month, and final results are usually achieved within 12 months.

items.question: Is hair transplant permanent?
items.answer: Yes. Hair follicles taken from the donor area are genetically resistant to hair loss and continue to grow permanently.

items.question: Can women have a hair transplant?
items.answer: Yes, women who are suitable candidates can also undergo hair transplantation after medical evaluation.

items.question: When can I return to work?
items.answer: Most patients can return to social life within 2–3 days. Office workers may return even sooner.

items.question: Is there an age limit?
items.answer: Generally suitable for individuals over 18 whose hair loss pattern has largely stabilized.

items.question: Can hair transplant be done in summer?
items.answer: Yes, but protection from direct sunlight is essential.

items.question: Can I exercise after the procedure?
items.answer: Heavy exercise should be avoided for the first 2 weeks. Light walking is possible after a few days.

items.question: Can transplanted hair be cut?
items.answer: Yes. Transplanted hair grows naturally and can be cut and styled as desired.

items.question: Will there be scars?
items.answer: With FUE and Sapphire techniques, scarring is minimal. Micro marks in the donor area are usually not visible once hair grows.
